testcase.name: ard_stack_adding_missing_attributes

failure.message: Expected `... <- NULL` not to throw any errors. ('test-ard_stack.R:121:3')

testcase.failure: Expected `... <- NULL` not to throw any errors. Actually got a <rlang_error> with message: `any_of()` must be used within a *selecting* function. i See <https://tidyselect.r-lib.org/reference/faq-selection-context.html> for details. Backtrace: ▆ 1. ├─testthat::expect_error(...) at test-ard_stack.R:121:3 2. │ └─testthat:::expect_condition_matching_(...) 3. │ └─testthat:::quasi_capture(...) 4. │ ├─testthat (local) .capture(...) 5. │ │ └─base::withCallingHandlers(...) 6. │ └─rlang::eval_bare(quo_get_expr(.quo), quo_get_env(.quo)) 7. └─cards::ard_stack(...) 8. ├─cards::bind_ard(...) 9. │ └─dplyr::bind_rows(...) 10. │ └─rlang::list2(...) 11. ├─cards::ard_missing(data = data, by = any_of(.by), variables = all_of(variables)) 12. ├─cards:::ard_missing.data.frame(...) 13. └─tidyselect::any_of(.by) 14. ├─vars %||% peek_vars(fn = "any_of") 15. └─tidyselect::peek_vars(fn = "any_of") 16. └─cli::cli_abort(...) 17. └─rlang::abort(...)

testcase.name: ard_stack_adding_missing_attributes

error.message: Error in `eval(code, test_env)`: object 'ard_test' not found ('test-ard_stack.R:133:3')

testcase.error: Error in `eval(code, test_env)`: object 'ard_test' not found Backtrace: ▆ 1. └─testthat::expect_equal(...) at test-ard_stack.R:133:3 2. └─testthat::quasi_label(enquo(object), label) 3. └─rlang::eval_bare(expr, quo_get_env(quo))

skipped.message: Reason: package_version(paste(R.version$major, R.version$minor, sep = ".")) <= package_version("4.5.0") is not TRUE ('test-ard_strata.R:76:3')

testcase.name: ard_tabulate_rows_works
testcase.error: Error: object 'TRTA' not found Backtrace: ▆ 1. └─cards::ard_tabulate_rows(ADSL, by = TRTA) 2. ├─cards::ard_tabulate_value(...) 3. └─cards:::ard_tabulate_value.data.frame(...)

skipped.message: Reason: package_version(paste(R.version$major, R.version$minor, sep = ".")) <= package_version("4.5.0") is not TRUE ('test-ard_tabulate_value.R:41:3')

testcase.name: compare_ard_errors_when_keys_are_empty
testcase.system-out: Subsetting quosures with `[[` is deprecated as of rlang 0.4.0 Please use `quo_get_expr()` instead. Backtrace: ▆ 1. ├─testthat::expect_error(compare_ard(ard1, ard2, keys = "nonexistent_col")) at test-compare_ard.R:111:3 2. │ └─testthat:::expect_condition_matching_(...) 3. │ └─testthat:::quasi_capture(...) 4. │ ├─testthat (local) .capture(...) 5. │ │ └─base::withCallingHandlers(...) 6. │ └─rlang::eval_bare(quo_get_expr(.quo), quo_get_env(.quo)) 7. └─cards::compare_ard(ard1, ard2, keys = "nonexistent_col") 8. └─cards:::.process_keys_arg(...) 9. └─cards::cards_select(...) 10. └─base::tryCatch(...) 11. └─base (local) tryCatchList(expr, classes, parentenv, handlers) 12. └─base (local) tryCatchOne(expr, names, parentenv, handlers[[1L]]) 13. └─value[[3L]](cond) 14. ├─base::tryCatch(...) 15. │ └─base (local) tryCatchList(expr, classes, parentenv, handlers) 16. │ └─base (local) tryCatchOne(expr, names, parentenv, handlers[[1L]]) 17. │ └─base (local) doTryCatch(return(expr), name, parentenv, handler) 18. ├─base::eval(as.list(enexpr)[[1]]) 19. ├─as.list(enexpr)[[1]] 20. └─rlang:::`[[.quosure`(as.list(enexpr), 1) 21. └─rlang:::deprecate_soft(...) 22. └─rlang:::.rlang_lifecycle_deprecate_warn0(...)

testcase.name: compare_ard_errors_when_columns_argument_selects_mismatched_columns
testcase.system-out: Subsetting quosures with `[[` is deprecated as of rlang 0.4.0 Please use `quo_get_expr()` instead. Backtrace: ▆ 1. ├─testthat::expect_error(compare_ard(ard1, ard2, columns = "stat_label")) at test-compare_ard.R:283:3 2. │ └─testthat:::expect_condition_matching_(...) 3. │ └─testthat:::quasi_capture(...) 4. │ ├─testthat (local) .capture(...) 5. │ │ └─base::withCallingHandlers(...) 6. │ └─rlang::eval_bare(quo_get_expr(.quo), quo_get_env(.quo)) 7. └─cards::compare_ard(ard1, ard2, columns = "stat_label") 8. └─cards:::.process_compare_arg(...) 9. └─cards::cards_select(...) 10. └─base::tryCatch(...) 11. └─base (local) tryCatchList(expr, classes, parentenv, handlers) 12. └─base (local) tryCatchOne(expr, names, parentenv, handlers[[1L]]) 13. └─value[[3L]](cond) 14. ├─base::tryCatch(...) 15. │ └─base (local) tryCatchList(expr, classes, parentenv, handlers) 16. │ └─base (local) tryCatchOne(expr, names, parentenv, handlers[[1L]]) 17. │ └─base (local) doTryCatch(return(expr), name, parentenv, handler) 18. ├─base::eval(as.list(enexpr)[[1]]) 19. ├─as.list(enexpr)[[1]] 20. └─rlang:::`[[.quosure`(as.list(enexpr), 1) 21. └─rlang:::deprecate_soft(...) 22. └─rlang:::.rlang_lifecycle_deprecate_warn0(...)

testcase.name: filter_ard_hierarchical_error_messaging_works
testcase.failure: Snapshot of code has changed: old[3:6] vs new[3:6] n > 10) Condition Error in `filter_ard_hierarchical()`: - ! Filtering is only available for stacked hierarchical ARDs created using `ard_stack_hierarchical()` or `ard_stack_hierarchical_count()`. + ! No statistics available in the ARD for variable "AGEGR1". In order to filter on "AGEGR1" it must be specified in the `include` argument when the ARD is created. * Run `testthat::snapshot_accept("filter_ard_hierarchical", "testthat")` to accept the change. * Run `testthat::snapshot_review("filter_ard_hierarchical", "testthat")` to review the change.

testcase.name: sort_ard_hierarchical_error_messaging_works

failure.message: Expected `sort_ard_hierarchical(ard_tabulate(ADSL, by = "ARM", variables = "AGEGR1"))` to throw a error. ('test-sort_ard_hierarchical.R:425:3')

testcase.failure: Expected `sort_ard_hierarchical(ard_tabulate(ADSL, by = "ARM", variables = "AGEGR1"))` to throw a error. Backtrace: ▆ 1. └─testthat::expect_snapshot(...) at test-sort_ard_hierarchical.R:425:3 2. └─testthat:::expect_snapshot_(...)

testcase.name: sort_ard_hierarchical_error_messaging_works
testcase.failure: Snapshot of code has changed: old vs new Code - sort_ard_hierarchical(ard_tabulate(ADSL, by = "ARM", variables = "AGEGR1")) + sort_ard_hierarchical(ard, sort = "no_sorting") Condition Error in `sort_ard_hierarchical()`: - ! Sorting is only available for stacked hierarchical ARDs created using `ard_stack_hierarchical()` or `ard_stack_hierarchical_count()`. + ! Sorting type must be either "descending" or "alphanumeric" for all variables. * Run `testthat::snapshot_accept("sort_ard_hierarchical", "testthat")` to accept the change. * Run `testthat::snapshot_review("sort_ard_hierarchical", "testthat")` to review the change.

testcase.name: sort_ard_hierarchical_error_messaging_works
testcase.failure: Snapshot of code has changed: old vs new Code - sort_ard_hierarchical(ard, sort = "no_sorting") + sort_ard_hierarchical(ard) Condition Error in `sort_ard_hierarchical()`: - ! Sorting type must be either "descending" or "alphanumeric" for all variables. + ! If `sort='descending'` for any variables then either "n" or "p" must be present in `x` for each of these specified variables in order to calculate the count sums used for sorting. * Run `testthat::snapshot_accept("sort_ard_hierarchical", "testthat")` to accept the change. * Run `testthat::snapshot_review("sort_ard_hierarchical", "testthat")` to review the change.
